message:
refactor(pages/wizard): extract UTF-8-safe sample_title truncation + two regression tests

The \`sample_titles\` rendering in the pages wizard had a 6-line
inline closure that truncated titles longer than 30 chars using
\`floor_char_boundary(27)\`. It worked but was duplicated in spirit
by other truncation call sites and was awkward to unit-test.

Extract the closure into a free function
\`truncate_sample_title(&str) -> String\` at module scope so the
truncation contract can be exercised in isolation, and replace the
inline closure with \`.map(|t| truncate_sample_title(t))\`. Pure
refactor — behavior-preserving, byte-for-byte equivalent output.

Add two regression tests under \`mod tests\`:

- \`sample_title_truncation_is_utf8_boundary_safe\`
  Pins both the ASCII and the multi-byte paths: a 36-char ASCII
  title truncates to exactly 27 chars + \"...\", a title composed
  of a long run of \"日本語\" (3 bytes/char) plus a suffix both
  ends in \"...\" AND lands on a UTF-8 char boundary (checked via
  \`is_char_boundary(len())\`) AND stays within the 30-char budget.
  Catches any future regression that naively truncates on byte 27
  and panics on a multi-byte codepoint, or that silently extends
  past the budget.

- \`wizard_state_debug_redacts_sensitive_fields\`
  Constructs a \`WizardState\` populated with a password, a
  recovery_secret byte vector, and a cloudflare_api_token, then
  formats the state via \`format!(\"{state:?}\")\`. Asserts the
  debug output:
  - keeps the field NAMES visible (so operators can tell which
    fields exist when tailing logs),
  - substitutes \"[REDACTED]\" for their VALUES,
  - never surfaces the underlying password / token / byte
    sequence.
  Pins the Debug-redaction contract against anyone who later
  regenerates Debug with \`#[derive(Debug)]\` or adds a new
  sensitive field and forgets the custom redaction impl.

Pure test + extract-helper change — no UX or wire-format impact.
